txt: Yeldard, Arthur (Yeldar or Yelder), sizar of Clare Hall, Cambridge, 1544, B.A. 1547-8, fellow of Pembroke Hall 1550, and M.A. 1552, an original fellow Trinity Coll. (Oxford) 1556; incorporated 12 Nov., 1556, B.D. supd. 21 June, 1557, admitted 26 June, 1563, D.D. 18 Feb., 1565-6, (second) president Trinity Coll. 1559-98, vice-chancellor 1580; born at Houghton Strother, near the Tyne, Northumberland, and educated as a chorister in the Benedictine convent of Durham, became a master in Rotheram college, Yorkshire, rector of Garsington, Oxon, 1563, vicar of Much Waltham, Essex, 1572; died 1 or 2 Feb., 1598-9, buried in the college chapel, will at Oxford proved 16 April, 1599. See Ath. i. 674; Gutch, i. 520 and 534; Cooper, ii. 267; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Perte, John (or Part), an original scholar of Trinity Coll. 1555 from co. Warwick; B.A. 8 May, 1556, fellow 1556. See Gutch, i. 518, 520.
Harris, John, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1556, from co. Gloucester, B.A. 8 May, 1559, fellow 1559, 12 years in medicine, sup. for B.Med. 1 June, 1575. See Gutch, i. 520.
Saltmarsh, William (Saltmersh), scholar Trinity Coll. 1556, B.A. 23 May, 1558, M.A. 1 Dec., 1562. See Gutch, i. 520.
Sotherne, Richard, scholar Trinity Coll. 7 June, 1558, aged 16, B.A. 28 May, 1560, fellow Exeter Coll. 1560-62. See Boase, 42.

Allen, Thomas, scholar Trinity Coll. 4 June, 1561, B.A. 13 May, 1563, fellow 1565-70, M.A. 21 April, 1567, of Gloucester Hall 1570, an eminent antiquary, philosopher, and mathematician, died in Gloucester Hall 30 Sept., 1632, aged 90, buried in chapel of Trinity College. See Gutch, i. 534; O.H.S., i. 251; Ath., ii. 541; & Hearne, iii. 78.

Johnson, Robert, B.A. from Clare Coll., Cambridge, 1560-1, and fellow Trinity Coll. 1563, M.A. 1564 (incorporated 20 Feb., 1564-5), B.D. 1571, chaplain to Sir Nicholas Bacon, lord keeper; canon of Peterborough 1569-75, of Norwich, 1570-6, and of Rochester 1570, minister of St. Clement s, London, about 1573, and in the same year a prisoner for nonconformity in the Gatehouse, Westminster, where he died (shortly after) 16 May, 1574. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 67; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; Fasti, i. 165; & Cooper, i. 323.

Chamberlaine, Bartholomew (Chamber), scholar of Trinity Coll. 7 June, 1563, aged 17; B.A. 5 July, 1566, fellow 1567, M.A. 4 Dec., 1570, sup. for licence to preach 1 June, 1576, B.D. 3 July, 1576, D.D. 19 Jan., 1578-9, incorp. at Cambridge 1585, rector of Abbot's Morton, co. Worcester, 1577, vicar of Burford, Oxon, 1578, rector of Holywell, Hunts, 1579, of Sandy, Beds, 1593, and of Hemingford Abbots, Hunts, 1600. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Ath., i. 584.
Wilson, Roger, scholar Trinity Coll. 1563, B.A. 5 July, 1566, fellow 1569, M.A. 4 Dec., 1570, B.D. sup. 14 July, 1579; vicar of Kelloe, co. Durham, 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Campion, Edmund, fellow Trinity Coll. 1563, B.A. 13 May, 1563, M.A. 21 April, 1567, vicar of Sherbourne, co. Gloucester, 1568. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Forde, Thomas (Foorde), B.A. 13 May, 1563, fellow of Trinity Coll. 1564, M.A. 21 April, 1567. Went to Douay and on his return to England was hanged at Tyburn 28 May, 1582. See Boase, 32; & D.N.B.
Ellis, Anthony (Ellys), scholar of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564; B.A. 5 July, 1566, vicar of Radborne Cheney, co. Warwick, 1573. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. i. 264.
Michell, John, commoner of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564; B.A. 21 April, 1567, fellow Lincoln Coll. 1567-75, M.A. 31 July, 1573. See O.H.S. i. 264.
Mathewe, George (als. Chamberlaine), commoner of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564, scholar 1565, B.A. 11 May, 1569, fellow 1569, M.A. 24 Nov., 1573.
Chamberlaine, Robert, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564, and of Lincoln's Inn 1568. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Day, Henry, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564, scholar 1565, B.A. 11 May, 1569, fellow 1569, M.A. 24 May, 1573.
Arden, John, commoner of Trinity Coll. in or before 1564. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bassett, Richard, B.A. 29 March, 1561, M.A. 3 Feb., 1564-5, fellow Trinity Coll. about 1564.
Orpwoode, Thomas, B.A. and fellow Trinity Coll. in and before 1564. See O.H.S. i. 251.
Maude, Barnard, scholar Trinity Coll. in and before 1564, B.A. 5 July, 1566.
Aynscombe, Thomas (Einscomm), commoner of Trinity Coll. in or before 1564.
Chamberlaine, Walter, commoner of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Cloudesleie, Thomas, commoner of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Crispin, Nicholas, scholar of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Richardlye, John, commoner of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Limbericke, Thomas, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Gre(e)ne, James, commoner of Trinity Coll. in or before 1564.
Limbericke, James, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Martin, Richard, scholar of Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Yeldarde, Richard, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.
Legate, John, commoner Trinity Coll. in and before 1564.

Wethered, Thomas (Wetherhood), scholar Trinity Coll. 1565, B.A. 14 Dec., 1568, sup. for M.A. Dec., 1572; rector of Binbrook St. Mary, co. Lincoln, 1566, or of Aston Clinton, Bucks, 1571, but see Foster's Index Eccl.
Ashbroke, John, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1566, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2. See O.H.S., i. 283; xii. 12.
Chew, John (Chaw), scholar Trinity Coll. 1567, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1572, M.A. 14 May, 1575.

Legge, Thomas, 2s. Stephen, of Norwich; pensioner of Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, Oct., 1552, scholar of Trinity Coll., 1555, B.A. 1556-7, fellow and M.A. 1560, fellow of Jesus Coll. 1568, LL.D. 1575, supl. for incorporation at Oxford 1586, (the 2nd) master of Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1573, vice-chancellor 1587, regius professor of Hebrew in that university circa 1563-73, an advocate of Doctors' Commons 16 May, 1590, a master in chancery about 1595; died 12 July, 1607, aged 72. See Cooper, ii. 454; Fasti, i. 239; & Coote's Civilians, 60.
Warren, Thomas, scholar Trinity Coll. 1568, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1572, M.A. 14 May, 1575; buried in the college chapel 28 April, 1598; admon. at Oxford next day. See Gutch, i. 534.
Atherton, John, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1568, B.A. sup. 26 April, 1571. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., i.

Withers, Henry, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1564-5, M.A. 1568, fellow Trinity Coll. 1569; incorporated 12 July, 1569. B.D. (Oxford) 26 Jan., 1573-4, incorporated at Cambridge 1587, as D.D. Wittenburgh 1592, incorporated at Oxford 15 July, 1595, "exhibition given to Henry Withers, son of John Withers, late citizen and merchant taylor, deceased, now student within Peter House, in the university of Cambridge," 1564 (Robinson, i. 7); vicar of Kensington, Middlesex, 1572, rector of Eynesford, Kent, 1573, of St. Martin Outwich, London, 1574, and of TheydonGarnon, Essex 1591. See O.H.S. x. 350; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Cottington, James, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1569, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1572, M.A. 14 May, 1575, B.D. 2 March, 1579-80, D.D. 13 March, 1581-2, vicar of East Brent, Somerset, 1576, archdeacon of Surrey 1580, rector of Ditcheat, Somerset, 1582, precentor of Wells 1583, and canon 1584, rector of Yeovilton 1584, died in 1605. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., xii. 12.

Pattinson, Richard (Pattynson), scholar Trinity Coll. 1569, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1572; perhaps B.D. 6 July, 1585, vicar of Braintree, Essex, 1577-85, rector of Little Waltham, Essex, 1585, until his death in 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pickering, Matthew, scholar Trinity Coll. 1569, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, vicar of Bathampton, Somerset, 1571, and rector of Hinton Blewett, Somerset, 1577. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Langston, Francis (Lanstone), scholar of Trinity Coll. 1569; B.A. 12 June, 1573, fellow 1577, M.A. 26 June, 1578, B.C.L. 5 Nov., 1588. See O.H.S. xii. 28.
Swinborne, Ralph, scholar Trinity Coll. 1569, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1572, M.A. 8 July, 1575, suspected of being a papist. See O.H.S. i. 28
Yates, Bartholomew (Yeates), scholar Trinity Coll. 1569, B.A. 12 June, 1573, fellow 1574, M.A. 21 May, 1577. See O.H.S. xii. 28.

Potter, Richard, scholar Trinity Coll. 1571, from Oxon, B.A. 24 May, 1574, M.A. 7 July, 1578 (as Porter), fellow 1579, B.D. 6 July, 1587, vicar of Mere, Wilts, 1586-1630, rector of Kilmington, Somerset, 1598-1626, canon of Worcester 1598, died 1628; father of Francis 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chamberlayne, John, scholar Trinity Coll. 1571, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, fellow 1573, M.A. 24 May, 1576. See O.H.S., xii. 12; & D.N.B., x. 2.

Blounte, George, of co. Worcester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 17, 'Mro. Hindmer tutori gram'; fellow of New Coll. 1577, B.A. 27 May, 1581, M.A. 25 Nov., 1585, one of these names vicar of Romsey and rector of Exton, Hants, 1589, and rector of Weyhill 1593 (as Blunt). See Christopher 1572, and also Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Laurens, Thomas, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1576-80, fellow 1580-88, B.A. 3 Nov., 1582, M.A. 22 June, 1587; one of these names beneficed in Wilts and Berks; perhaps father of Thomas 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Bloxam, iv. 200.

Cosens, John, of Devon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 20; 'Dno. Matthewe tutori dial,' scholar 1574, B.A. 17 May, 1576, fellow 1576, M.A. 23 Oct., 1579, sup. for licence to practice medicine 9 May, 1585, sup. for B.Med. 9 June, 1585.

Garthwaite, Giles (Gawthwithe or Garethwicke), of Richmondshire, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date, 12 Dec., 1572, aged 16; "Mro. Hindmer tutori gram.," B.A. 29 Feb. 1575-6 (as Cartwright), vicar of Heighington, co. Durham, 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ridley, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 19; "Mro. Hindmer tutori dialect.," scholar 1572, chaplain Magdalen Coll. 1574-6, B.A. 24 May, 1574. See Roger, 1575; & Bloxam, ii. 128.

Martin, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18; (Mro. Chamberlaine tutori dial.); B.A. 3 March, 1573-4; possibly bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1584. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Scudamore, Rowland (Skidmer), of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 19, "Dno. Day tutori dial.;" and of Balliol Coll. 1575, aged 22 (as Skidmore); exhibitioner 1574-8, B.A. 28 May, 1576.

Cecill, John, of co. Worcester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 16; 'Mro. Chamberlaine Tutori dial,' scholar 1573, B.A. 17 May, 1576, fellow 1576, M.A. 14 March, 1579-80.
Dade, Arthur, of Devon. Trinity Coll., matric.entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18, 'Dno. Mathewe tutori dial;' rector of Compton Abbas, Dorset, 1584. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Erington, George, of Northumberland, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18; "Mro. Hindmer tutori gram."; B.A. 29 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 23 March, 1578-9.
Crane, John (or Atkinson), of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 19; 'Mro. Hindmer tutori dialect,' scholar 1572, B.A. 24 May, 1574.
Anne, Richard (Ane), of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 15 (?16); 'Mro. Chamberlaine Tutori dial.' See O.H.S., i.
Pegge, Christopher, of Derby, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 20; "Mro. Sutton tutori Dial.," B.A. supd. 16 April, 1572.
Fletcher, Owen, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 21; "Dno. Day tutori dial.," scholar 1572, B.A. 24 May, 1574.
Allen, Richard, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1572, B.A. 17 May, 1576, fellow 1578, M.A. 21 April, 1580. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Gray's Inn Reg.
Hughes, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 19; "Dno. Chewe tutori dial.," B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6.
Haynes, George, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 20; "Dno. Chewe tutori dial." B.A. 16 May, 1580.
Milwarde, John (or Thomas), of co. Derby, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18; "Dno. Day, tutori dial."
Cooke, George (Coocke), of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 13; 'Mro. Chamberlaine Tutori gram.'
Fettiplace, Christopher, of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll. matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 15; "Mro. Chamberlaine Tutori dial."
Ashfeilde, Edmund, of Bucks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 12, 'Mro. Chamberlaine, Tutori Gram.'
Chamberlen, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 17; 'Mro. Chamberlaine Tutori gram.'
Atkins, John, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1572, B.A. 12 June, 1573, fellow 1574, M.A. 21 May, 1577. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ham(p)den, Edmund, of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18, "Mro. Chamberlaine tutori gram."
Griffithe, Courtney, of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 19; "Mro. Wilson tutori dial."
Hancocke, William, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 17, "Dno. Cotington tutori gram."
Poulet, Hugh, of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 14; "Mro. Hindmer tutori dialect."
Se(a)rle, Morris, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 18, "Mro. Cotington tutori gram."
(Wilkins?), Edward, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 24, "Mro. Wilson tutori Dial."
Nightingale, James, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 20; "Dno. Chewe tutori dial."
Barrie, John, of Devon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 17; 'Mro. Hindmer tutori gram.'
Tanner, John, of Essex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 12 Dec., 1572, aged 16, "Domino Daye tutori gram."

Bragge, Edward, of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 1573, aged 18; B.A. from Hart Hall 23 Feb., 1578-9, M.A. 30 June, 1581, canon of Chichester 1590. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Barnes, Thomas, of co. Worcester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 1573, aged 18.

Wright, Robert, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 14, scholar 1574, B.A. 13 June, 1580, fellow 1581, M.A. 7 July, 1584, B.D. 6 April, 1592, D.D. 12 July, 1597, licenced to preach 1605; warden of Wadham Coll. April-July, 1613, rector of St. John Evangelist 1589-90, of St. Katherine, Coleman Street, London, 1591, of Woodford, Essex, 1589-1619, and of Brixton Deverell, Wilts, 1596, chaplain to Q. Elizabeth, rector of Bourton-on-the-Water, co. Gloucester, 1625, and of Hayes, Middlesex, 1601, canon residentiary and treasurer of Wells 1601-32, vicar of Sonning, Berks, 1604, chaplain in ordinary to Q. Elizabeth and to James I., rector of Rattendon, Essex, 1619, bishop of Bristol 1623-32, and of Lichfield and Coventry 1632, until his death at Eccleshall Aug. or Sept., 1643; father of Calvert 1634. See Ath. iv. 800; Lansdowne MS. 985, f. 38; Fasti, i. 215; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Hobby, (Sir) Edward (Hobbie), of Berks, eq. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 14; B.A. 19 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 3 July, 1576 (s. of Sir Thomas, of Bisham, Berks), knighted 22 May, 1582, admitted to the Middle Temple 24 Jan., 1583-4, M.P. Queenborough 1586-7, Berks 1588-9, Kent 1593, Rochester 1597-8, 1601, 1604-11, and 1614; died 1 March, 1616-17; brother of Thomas P. 1574. See Ath. ii. 194; Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; D.N.B.; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Atkins, Henry, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 19; B.A. 10 June, 1575, M.A. 25 Feb., 1577-8 (son of Richard Atkins, of Great Berkhampstead, Herts, gent.), D.Med. Nantes, fellow of College of Physicians 1588, president 1606-8, 1616-17, & 1624-5, physician-in-ordinary for the space of 32 years to King James and King Charles, said to have declined the first patent for a baronetcy, died in Warwick Court, London, 21 Sept., 1635. See Munk's Roll, i. 93.

Bourne, John, of London, gent, Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 19; B.A. 19 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 10 June, 1577, B. & D.D. 31 March, 1596, rector of Wythycomb, Somerset, 1581, treasurer of Wells and canon 1584, rector of Hornblotton 1587, of Saltford 1588, and of Yeovilton 1591-1616, and vicar of Stogumber 1621-2. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus,

Warforde, William, of Bristol, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 13, scholar 1576, B.A. 22 March, 1577-8, fellow 1578, M.A. 30 March, 1582; entered the Society of Jesus at Rome in 1594; died at Valladolid in Spain 3 Nov., 1608, called Walford in Fasti, i. 206. See Ath. ii. 45; & Foley's Records S.J. iv. 574.

Avys, Robert, of Northants, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 16; scholar 1576, B.A. 22 March, 1577-8 (as Davys), fellow 1580, M.A. 4 July, 1582. B.D. 7 July, 1591, licence to preach 22 Feb., 1604-5, vicar of Watlington, Oxon, 1589. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., xii. 74.

Blount, (Sir) Thomas Pope (Blunte), of Herts. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 18 (? 16); B.A. 29 Feb., 1575 6, of Tittenhanger, Herts (son of William, of Osbaston, co. Leicester), knighted at Theobald's 7 May, 1603, died 10 Jan., 1638-9, father of the next named and of Henry 1615.

Hitchcocke, Thomas, of London, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 17; B.A. 1 Feb., 1577-8, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1587, treasurer 1615, perhaps M.P. Bishops Castle 1614; father of the next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wyvill, Christopher, of Richmondshire. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 14; of Constable Burton, Yorks (s. Sir Marmaduke); buried at Masham 22 July, 1614, probably identical with the next man. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Carricke, Richard (Carrique), of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 15; sometime of Clifford's Inn, and of Inner Temple 1583, as Caryque, father of Richard 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Campion, William, of Northants, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 19; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 10 July, 1579, M.A. 6 July, 1582, rector of Holcott, Northants, 1581. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lawson, Raynolde, of Yorks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 18 ("Mro. Hindmer tutori gram."); possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1578, as Reginald, of Staple Inn. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Repington, (Sir) John, of co. Warwick. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 14, B.A. 13 June, 1580; of Amington, co. Warwick (s. Thomas), knighted in Aug., 1624; brother of Edward 1598, and Humphry.

Fryer, Edward (Friar), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 10; "Gram. Mro. chamberlain tutori"; possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1582, as Edmund. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Holcombe, Edward (Holcum), of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 14, and again in 1579, then of Hart Hall, aged 18; B.A. from New Coll. 14 March, 1580-1, M.A. 10 July, 1584.
Osbaldeston, George (Osbarston), of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 11; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 31 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 22 June, 1584.
Bassan, Baptist, of London, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 16; admitted to Gray's Inn in 1584, from Staple Inn. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Pricket, Augustine, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 16; "Gram. Mro. Chamberlaine tutori" — of Exeter Coll. circa 1572, aged 15.
Rodney, Henry, of Somerset, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 11; probably eldest son of Morris, of Stoke Gifford, and brother of Thomas.
Rodney, Thomas, of Somerset, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 10; probably 2nd son of Morris, of Stoke Gifford, and brother of Henry.
Lassells, Christopher, of Richmondshire. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 20. See Yorkshire Visitation, ed. Foster, 61.
Seale, Richard, of co. Warwick. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 19, "Serviens Mri. Sanforde," B.A. 5 March, 1577-8.
Fre(e)man, Richard, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 17; "Dial. Mro. Chamberlein tutori."
Finimore, John (Fenimore), of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 19; "Gram. Mro. Chamberlein tutori."
Cæserson, John (Ceserson), of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 16; 'Dial. Mro. Hindmer tutori.'
Sandy, John, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 20; scholar 1575, B.A. 22 March, 1577-8.
Lassells, Francis, of Richmondshire. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 18; brother of Christopher and Roger.
Dringe, Jasper, of Berks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 17; 'Gram. Mro. Chamberlein, tutori.'
Cave, Jerome, of Norfolk, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 9; 'Gram. Mro. Chamberlein tutori.'
Collier, John, of Dorset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 17; 'Dial. Dno. Cottington tutori.'
Thompson, Francis, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 17, "Dial, Mro. Wilson, tutori."
Chamberlen, John, of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 10; 'Gram. Mro. Wilson tutori.'
Mo(o)re, John, of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 14; "Gram. Mro. Hindmer, tutori."
Swinburne, John, of Hunts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 22, "Dial. Mro. Wilson tutori."
Childe, William, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 16; 'Dial. Mro. Day tutori.'
Greenfeild, Edward (Grenefilde), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 17.
Jordan, Thomas (Jourden), of Sussex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 17.
Fitche, Rayman, of co. Warwick, gen. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 20.
Gifforde, Richard, of co. Worcester, Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 15.
Catesby, Thomas, of Bristol, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 19.
Everet, Richard, of Herts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 18.
Drew, Robert (Drue), of Devon. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 11 Nov., 1574, aged 17.
Knight, John, B.A. (? from Trinity Coll.) 31 Jan., 1574-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1575, M.A. 17 Feb., 1578-9; perhaps rector of Tintinhull, Somerset, 1576, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Fisher, Timothy, B.A. from (? Trinity Coll.) 31 March, 1574; fellow of Magdalen Coll. 1575, M.A. 22 June, 1577, vicar of Tavistock, Devon, 1585. See O.H.S. xii. 41.
Bingley, James (Byngley), scholar Trinity Coll. 1576, B.A. 22 March, 1577-8, fellow 1580, M.A. 4 May, 1582, rector of Gumley, co. Leicester, 1591. See O.H.S., xii. 74.
Shirley, Anton, scholar Trinity Coll. 1576, B.A. 22 March, 1577-8, fellow 1579, M.A. supd. 9 April, 1582; "a native of Oxford." See Wood's B.A. MS. List.
Walford, William, scholar Trinity Coll. 1576. See Wardford.

St. John, (Sir) Oliver (Saint-Jhoane), of Wilts, arm. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. dated 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; B.A. 26 June, 1578; student of Lincoln's Inn 1580, formerly of New Inn (s. Nicholas, of Lydiard Tregoze), M.P. Cirencester 1593, and Portsmouth 1604, to Jan., 1607-8, knighted 28 Feb., 1600-1, master of the ordnance 1608, lorddeputy of Ireland 1616-22, created Viscount Grandison in Ireland 23 June, 1623, and Baron Tregoze in England 1626; died s.p. 30 Dec., 1630. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Walton, Dunstan, of Somerset, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 13, B.A. 12 Feb., 1579-80, fellow elect All Souls' Coll. 1580, but never admitted; student of Middle Temple 1584, as 2s. Richard, of Shapwick, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Edmonds, William (Edmund), of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; fellow of Merton Coll. 1580, B.A. 26 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 9 July, 1585, rector of Ibston, Oxon, 1587. See O.H.S. iv. 273; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Walton, Thomas, of Somerset, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16, B.A. 12 Feb., 1579-80; student of Middle Temple 1574, as son and heir of Thomas, of Lam, Somerset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Alvey, Arthur, of Hunts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; scholar 1577, B.A. 3 July, 1581, fellow 1583, M.A. 26 May, 1585, rector of Knotting, Beds, 1592. See O.H.S., xii. 96.

Feilde, Edward, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; B.A. 19 Oct., 1583, M.A. 30 June, 1586, rector of Drayton Beauchamp, Bucks, 1586. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Snowden, William (Snawden), of co. Durham, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18, B.A. 14 Feb., 1581-2; perhaps vicar of Leake, Yorks, 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.
T(h)omson, Edward, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17, scholar 1578, B.A. 3 July, 1581, fellow 1583, M.A. 26 May, 1585, B.D. 11 June, 1594.
Browne, Aaron, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 28 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. from New Coll. 7 July, 1586.
Wyvell, Christopher, of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 14, of Lincoln's Inn 1580, and 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Foster, Edward, of Berks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Amner, Thomas (or Anmer), of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Me(y)ricke, William, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.

Master, Robert, of London, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 14; fellow of All Souls' Coll., B.C.L. 14 July, 1590, D.C.L. 10 Dec., 1594 (as Masters); principal of St. Alban Hall 1599-1603, an advocate 1599, chancellor of Rochester and Lichfield, M.P. Cricklade 1601; died 10 July, 1625; probably brother of Henry Masters, see preceding page. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 64; Fasti, i. 267; & Coote's Civilians, 65.

Chamberlayn, Edward, of co. Gloucester, 'mil. fil.' Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16; (perhaps subscribed from Oriel Coll. 23 Nov., 1581), of Lincoln's Inn 1581. (Memo: Sir Thomas Chamberlaine, of Prestbury, co. Gloucester, was father of Edmund, of Maugersbury, who died 1634) brother of John same date, and of Thomas 1582, and father of John 1624. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Cuffe, Henry, s. Robert, of Donyatt, Somerset; scholar Trinity Coll. 1578, B.A. 13 June, 1580, fellow 1583; fellow Merton Coll. 1586, M.A. 20 Feb., 1588-9, regius professor of Greek 1590-7, proctor 1594, private secretary to the Earl of Essex, condemned as his accomplice and was hanged at Tyburn 1601. See Ath., i. 704; O.H.S., iv. 274; & D.N.B.

Chamberlayn, (Sir) John, (s. Thomas) of co. Gloucester, mil. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 19; of Prestbury, co. Gloucester, and knighted (? 6 Sept., 1599), died s.p., brother of Edward same date, and of Thomas 1582.

Fixer, John, of Hants, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18; scholar 1579, B.A. 7 July, 1582; "was received into the English college at Rome and became noted for his religion and learning." See Fasti, i. 221.

Bayliffe, Henry (Baliffe), of Wilts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 15; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1581, M.P. Malmesbury 1586-7, 1588-9, son of William, of Monkton, Wilts. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Masters, Henry, of London, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 13; B.A. 2 June, 1584, M.A. 12 July, 1589, principal of St. Alban Hall 1603-14 as Master; probably brother of Robert Master, see next page.
Oven, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18; scholar 1578, B.A. 13 June, 1580, fellow 1582, of Godstow, Oxon.
Slyfield, Henry (Sleyfild), of Surrey, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16. See Surrey Archæological Collections, v. 47.
Tomlinson, (—), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18, and also 3 April, 1579, aged 17.
Bedell, Richard (Bedal), of co. Stafford, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18; B.A. 11 Feb., 1584-5.
Lowndes, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 20.
Gwyn, Ellis, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16.

Kettell, Ralph, of Herts, gen. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 15; scholar 1579, B.A. 7 July, 1582, fellow 1583, M.A. 23 April, 1586, B.D. 11 June, 1594, D.D. 9 May, 1597, licenced to preach 29 Jan., 1604-5, president of his college 1599-1643, of Gray's Inn 1609, rector of Garsingdon, Oxon, 1588, and of Avington, Hants, 1597; died 1 July, 1643, huried in Garsingdon church; will at Oxford proved 8 May, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Blakiston, Marmaduke (Blackston), of co. Durham, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 16; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 11 July, 1581, M.A. (sup. 27 April) 1583, rector of Redmarshall, co. Durham, 1585, vicar of Woodhorne, Northumberland, 1597, rector of Sedgfield, co. Durham, 1602, archdeacon of the East Riding 1615-25, canon of York 1618-23, and of Durham 1620, resigned 1633. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Seller, John, scholar Trinity Coll. 1579, B.A. 12 Dec., 1583, fellow 1584, M.A. 31 May, 1587, B.D. 9 July, 1596, licenced to preach 16 Feb., 1603-4, then late fellow; rector of Ickford, Bucks, 1597, and perhaps rector of St. Mary Bishophill the 2nd, York, 1605; father of John 1609, and of Henry 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Blomer, (Sir) Henry, of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric-entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 14; B.A. from St. John's Coll., 4 July, 1582, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1591 (as son and heir of William, of Hatherop, co. Gloucester, esq.), knighted 23 July, 1603. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Egioke, George (Edgeocke), of co. Warwick, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 17; a student of Middle Temple 1580, as late of New Inn, gent.; M.P. Tamworth 1601; buried in St. Andrew's, Holborn, 1601. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Shaftoo, Christopher, of Northumberland, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 18; B.A. sup. 22 Feb., 1581-2, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1593, as of Newcastle-on-Tyne; perhaps brother of John 1580. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Trentham, Francis, of Northumberland, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16; perhaps student of Gray's Inn 1584, as of Rossiter, co. Stafford, (son of Thomas), and M.P. co. Stafford (Nov.) 1610-11. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Poole, (Sir) Henry, of Wilts, arm. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16; knighted 10 June, 1603, M.P. Cricklade 1604-11, Wilts 1614, Malmesbury 1621-2, Oxon 1624-5, Wilts 1626. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Scroope, Richard, of Hants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1583, as of Bucks, son of a bencher (Ralph); brother of Adrian 1577, and of the last-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Scroope, Ralph, of Hants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 14; student of Lincoln's Inn 1583, as of Bucks, son of a bencher (Ralph); brother of Adrian 1577, and of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Clopton, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 15; of the Inner Temple 1584, as of Bradwell, co. Gloucester, late of Clifford's Inn. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hungerford, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1591, perhaps of Stock, Wilts, M.P. Great Bedwin 1593. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Fettiplace, Denton (Feteplace), of Bucks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1581, as 2nd son of George, a bencher of that inn. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Norries, Edward, of co. Lancaster, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1583, as of Speke, co. Lancaster, son of Edward. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Stagge, William, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1584, as of Ashton, Dorset. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Claxton, Ralph, of co. Durham, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16, and perhaps also 10 April, 1581, then aged 20; brother of Cuthbert.
Wiseman, Thomas (Wyseman), of Essex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pinkney, Henry, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 15; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 27 April, 1586, M.A. 26 June, 1594.
Stampe, Edward, of Berks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Shafto, John, of Northumberland, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16; perhaps brother of Christopher.
Fitzjames, James (Fithes-james), of Somerset, gen. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 15.
Westwood, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 14.
Busby, Richard (Busbie), of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16.
Salisburie, Thomas, of co. Denbigh, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16.
Charnocke, Edward, of co. Lancaster, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 13.
Sherwood, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16.
Hobby, Anthony (Hobye), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 17.
Browne, Thomas, scholar of Trinity Coll. 1579, B.A. 12 Dec., 1583, fellow 1586. See O.H.S., xii. 115.
Minet, (—), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 13.
Re(a)de, John, of co. Lincoln, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 17.
Welbecke, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 19.
Poole, Walton, of Wilts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 15.
Sorel, John, of Essex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 29 Jan., 1579-80, aged 16.
Stampe, John, of Berks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 12.
Marten, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 3 April, 1579, aged 18.

Mervin, John (Marvin, or Marveon), of Wilts, eq. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 22; perhaps a student of Middle Temple 1583, as son of Sir Edmund, of Fonthill Giffard, M.P. Hindon 1586-9, and died 23 June, 1601. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Percivale, Geoffry, of Cheshire, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 18; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 20 Jan., 1582-3, fellow 1583, M.A. 8 July, 1585, B.D. 30 Jan., 1595-6, rector of Quinton, Northants, 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Corbette, Peter, of Salop, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1581, aged 15; of the Middle Temple 1584 (as 2nd son of Reginald, of Stoke, Salop, esq.), possibly brother of Raynold. See also Richard 1604 & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hutchins, George, of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 18; scholar 1580, B.A. 12 Dec., 1583, fellow 1584, M.A. 24 Nov., 1601; one of these names beneficed in Devon and Cornwall. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Flyer, Ralph, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 13; bar.-at-law, of Middle Temple, 1592 (as son and heir of Richard Fleyer, of Uttoxeter); died 16 Feb., 1643. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Annesley, Edmund (Ansley), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 14; possibly brother of John, 1585 (see Visitation of Oxon, 263), perhaps then son of Edmond, knighted at Whitehall 23 July, 1603.

Sneyde, William, of co. Stafford, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1584, as of Bradwall, co. Stafford; probably son of Ralph, and died 1613. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Packer, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1587, as son and heir of Edward, of Alston, co. Gloucester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Cave, John, of co. Stafford, gent. Trinity Coll., admission under date 20 Sept., 1574, aged 10, 'Gram. Mro. Chamberlein tutori'; matric. from St. Mary Hall 27 Nov., 1581, aged 17, B.A. 8 Feb., 1582-3.
Frauncis, William (Francis), of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17; B.A. 12 June, 1585, M.A. 3 Dec., 1589; perhaps vicar of Harlow, Essex, 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Woodforde, Robert, of Bucks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 16; perhaps bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1604, as of London. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Corbette, Richard, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.entry under date circa 1581, aged 17; possibly father of John (created a baronet 19 Sept., 1627) and of Richard 1604.
Claxton, Cuthbert, of co. Durham, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 15; perhaps son of William, of Wynyard, and brother of Ralph.
Alforde, Edward, of Middlesex, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 15. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Jones, Henry, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17, B.A. 12 June, 1585; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 3 July, 1588.
Jones, Richard, of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 17; scholar 1580, B.A. 7 Feb., 1583-4.
Bearde, William, of Oxon, 'eq. fil.' Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 30; B.A. 29 March, 1585, M.A. 13 Feb., 1587-8.
Wandesforde, Thomas, of Yorks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 16; brother of Christopher.
Munne, Nicholas, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 14; B.A. 18 Dec., 1587, M.A. 20 June, 1592.
Howe, William, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 16; B.A. 12 Dec., 1583.
Bedell, Richard (Beedle), of co. Worcester gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 20.
Myers, Marmaduke (Myerres), of co. Durham, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 20.
Egioke, "Rodolphus" (Egeoke or Edghocke), of co. Warwick, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 10 Feb. 1581-2, aged 15.
Warforde, Henry (Wareforde), of Bristol, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 11.
Layton, Thomas (Laitone), of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 19.
Savage, Francis, of co. Worcester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 17.
Wilkinson, William, of co. Durham, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 16.
Fitzwilliams, John, of Herts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 14.
Dakin, Robert, of co. Derby, gent. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 17.
Bennette, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 19.
Milwarde, Arnold, of Northants, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under 10 April, 1581, aged 14.
Morgan, William, of Dorset, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 16.
Cheyney, Henry, of Bucks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric.-entry under date 10 April, 1581, aged 18.
Robertes, Richard, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1581-2, aged 15.
Oven, Walter, of Oxon. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 1 Dec., 1581, aged 13, "serviens."
Askell, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 16.
Knight, Arcadius, of Hants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 20.

Smarte, Humfrey, of co. Warwick, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 27 April, 1582, aged 15; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 31 Jan., 1585-6, M.A. 12 July, 1589; vicar of Bicknor English, co. Gloucester, 1599-1630, and perhaps rector of Pwllcrochan 1630, and of Cronweare (both co. Pembroke) 1631, until his death about 1645, probably of the same family as Ezechias and Peter. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Adames, Barnard, of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1582-3, aged 17; scholar 1583, B.A. 17 Feb., 1586-7, fellow 1588, M.A. 21 Jan., 1590-1, bishop of Limerick 1603, and of Kilfenora 1606-17, preb. of Killaloe and vicar of Ballingarry in diocese of Limerick, died 22 March, 1605-6. See Cotton's Fasti Eccl. Hib., i. 383; & Ath., ii. 869.

Bowman, John, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1582-3, aged 17; B.A. 19 Oct., 1586, M.A. 21 Jan., 1590-1, B.D. 9 July, 1600, vicar of Harlow, Essex, 1601, and rector of Hartfield, Sussex, 1610, chancellor of St. Paul's 1615, until his death in 1629. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Shirley, John (Shyrleus), of co. Leicester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 20 April, 1582, aged 14; student of the Inner Temple 1584, as of Staunton Harold, co. Leicester, gent.; 2nd son of John, and brother of George 1573, and of Thomas 1588. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Feild, Francis, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1582-3, aged 18; B.A. 19 Oct., 1586, M.A. 21 Jan., 1590-1, B.D. 9 July, 1600, rector of Barkstone, co. Leicester, 1604, and of Middleton Stoney, Oxon, 1606. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ford, William, of Wilts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1582, aged 18; B.A. 5 July, 1586, rector of Castle Combe, Wilts, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ivie, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1582, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1585. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Nicholsone, Christopher, of co. Durham, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 18.
Hopton, Ralph, of co. Lincoln, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1582, aged 13.
Workman, Humphrey, of Wilts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 30 Aug., 1582, aged 19.

Lacon, (Sir) Francis, of Salop, arm. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 14; of Willey and Kinlet, Salop (s. Rowland, M.P.), student of Gray's Inn 1588, knighted by the Earl of Essex at Dublin Castle 13 July, 1599, M.P. Bridgnorth (Feb.) 1610-11; brother of Thomas 1583. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Ferrers, John, of co. Warwick, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1584 (s. Sir Humphrey), of Tamworth Castle; M.P. Tamworth 1586-7, 1593, 1604-11; knighted 7 May, 1603; died 17 Sept., 1640; brother of Edward 1591. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Smith, Richard, of Lincolnshire, student of Trinity Coll. about 1583, D.D. Valladolid in Spain, went into the mission of England, titular bishop of Chalcedon in Greece 1624, and sent to England; died in Paris 18 March, 1654-5, aged 88. See Ath. iii. 384.

Wenman, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1583-4, aged 18; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 8 May, 1587, (incorporated at Cambridge 1587), M.A. 19 Feb., 1590-1, dispd. towards B.D. 20 April, 1597, public orator 1594-7.

Hobby, William, of co. Gloucester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 16; student of Lincoln's Inn 1586; evidently brother of Giles Hoby same date and college. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Lacon, Thomas, of Salop, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 13; student of Inner Temple 1588, as of Kinlet, Salop; brother of Francis 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Binglye, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1583-4, aged 20; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 14 Jan., 1588-9, M.A. 23 Feb., 1592-3.
Hob(b)y, Giles, of co. Gloucester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 18; evidently brother of William Hobby same date and college.
Par(r), William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 14; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 17 Feb., 1583-4.
Buswell, Laurence, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 16; B.A. 8 July, 1590, as Boswell.
Croker, John, of co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1583-4, aged 16.
Ferrers, Thomas, of co. Warwick, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 16.
Fox, Nicholas, of Northants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 15.

Egioke, Francis (Egeocke), of co. Worcester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1584-5, aged 16; possibly a student of Middle Temple 1586, as son and heir of John, of Ipsley, co. Warwick, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Meyricke, Richard (Mericke), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1584, aged 20; student of Middle Temple 1587, as son and heir of Rowland, of Banbury, Oxon, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Clement, Richard, of co. Hereford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1584-5, aged 16; B.A. 3 June, 1589, M.A. 6 July, 1596. See William 1590.
Whitbrook, Thomas, of London, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1584-5, aged 18.
Orpudde, Christopher, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1584, aged 16.

Chaworth, George, of Notts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 4 March, 1585-6, aged 17; created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605, then a knight, admitted to Gray's Inn 1605 (as son and heir of John, of Southwell, Notts), knighted at Greenwich 29 May, 1605, M.P. Notts 1621-2, Arundel 1624, until declared void 24 March, 1624, created Baron and Viscount Chaworth 4 March, 1627-8, died 3 July, 1639. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Tapsell, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, B.A. 11 Feb., 1589-90; fellow Merton Coll. 1593, M.A. 2 July, 1597, B. and D.D. 7 July, 1617; a famous orator, rector of Spennithorne, Yorks, 1613-15, rector of St. Mary at Hill, London, 1616, until his death in 1637. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 144; & O.H.S. iv. 275.

Spenser, William, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 2 July, 1585, aged 13, B.A. 15 July, 1590, M.A. 3 July, 1593, B.D. 16 Feb., 1603-4, licenced to preach 4 Feb., 1604-5; rector of Radnage, Bucks, 1605-6, of East Hendred, Berks, July, 1606, admon. at Oxford 4 Aug., 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Annesley, John (Ansley), of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 17; B.A. 3 June, 1589, J. A. of Chipping Norton, Oxon (2s. of Sir Edmund), late of Clement's Inn, admitted to the Inner Temple 1590. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Martin, Henry, of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1598, as 5s. Adam, of Hinton St. George, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Osborne, Edward, of Bucks, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 9 July, 1585, aged 17; B.A. 3 June, 1589, M.A. 20 June, 1592, vicar of Shabbington, Bucks, 1590. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hanmer, John, of Salop, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 18; bar.-at-law, of Gray's Inn, 1592, as of Oswestry, Salop. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Randle, Giles, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 19.
Walton, William, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 9 July, 1585, aged 16.
Fox, John, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 12.
Thackam, Henry, of Wilts, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 4 Nov., 1586, aged 15, B.A. 16 Dec., 1590; rector of Freshford, Somerset, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Packer, Thomas, of Middlesex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1586, aged 18. See also John 1590.
Reade, John, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 16, B.A. 8 June, 1594.
Weare, Nicholas, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1586, aged 21, B.A. 6 July, 1590.
Hollier, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 16.

Jackson, Hugh, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 17; B.A. 15 July, 1592, perhaps rector of Brightlingsea, Essex, 1594, licenced 25 April, 1594, to marry Frances, daughter of James Roade, late of Roade, co. Chester, gent., deceased. See Foster's Index Eccl. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Hawker, John, of Wilts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1591, as son and heir of Hugh, of Heytesbury, Wilts. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Button, John, of Wilts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 19; one of these names vicar of Donhead St. Andrew, Wilts, 1593. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Gre(e)nhalgh, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 17.
Kettell, George, of Herts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 18.
Hawker, Thomas, of Wilts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1587-8, aged 15.

Fisher, Henry, of Devon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 May, 1588, aged 20; B.A. 16 Nov. 1591, M.A. 9 July, 1596; one of these names rector of St. Michael, Spurriergate, York, 1597, another vicar of Kidwelly, co. Carmarthen, 1597. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Orp(w)ood, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1588, aged 15; B.A. 20 Feb., 1592-3, M.A. 21 Nov., 1595, rector of Steventon, Hants, 1602; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lea, Walter, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Nov., 1588, aged 20; B.A. 16 Dec., 1590.
Waller, Thomas, of Bucks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1588, aged 16, B.A. 15 July, 1592.

Packer, John, of Middlesex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 March, 1589-90, aged 20; of Westminster, a clerk of the privy seal; M.P. West Looe 1628-9; the friend of Elliot, and secretary to Villiers, Duke of Buckingham; and possibly father of Robert, Philip, John, and George. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Eaton, John, of Kent, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 15, exhibitioner 1590, B.A. 16 Feb., 1595-6, M.A. 7 July, 1603 (then in orders), "a grand antinomian," licenced 26 Dec., 1621, to marry Anne, widow of — Crosman, late of Ipswich, Suffolk, clerk; vicar of Wickham Market, Suffolk, about 1604. until deprived 1619. See Ath. iii. 21; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & D.N.B.

Marmyon, Shakerley, of Northants, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1597, as of Burley-on-the-Hill, Northants, late of Clifford's Inn (s. Thomas, of Aynho, Northants), baptised at Aynho 23 Nov., 1575; father of Shakerley. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rake, John, of co. Derby, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 19, B.A. 21 June, 1591; perhaps rector of Penselwood, Somerset, 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Appletre, Anthony, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 17, B.A. 8 Feb., 1593-4; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 6 July, 1598.
Evans, Philip, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 15; B.A. from Merton Coll. 30 May, 1593.
Greaves, John (Greives), of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 17; B.A. 8 Feb., 1593-4.
Clement, William, of Hereford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 16. See Richard 1585.
Cade, John, of Herts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 14. See William 1579.
Adams, Thomas (or Addams), of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 19.
Shippye, George, of Cheshire (?), pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 15.
Lowe, Roger, of co. Hereford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 11.
Aish, Thomas (Ayshe), of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 22.
Tippinge, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 16.
Hawtrey, John, of Middlesex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1590, aged 18.
Howse, William, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 13.

Banberye, Edward, of Middlesex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 8 July, 1591, aged 15; B.A. (sup. 7 Feb.), 1593-4, M.A. 9 July, 1596, a student of the Inner Temple 1596, as of Stepney, Middlesex. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Lu(c)kyn, Walter, of Essex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 20; student of Inner Temple 1593, as of Baddow, Essex, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Thatcher, Robert, of co. Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18, B.A. 9 July, 1596.
Smith, Walter (Smyth), of Middx., gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 18.

Moore, Walter, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 April, 1592, aged 15; B.A. 8 Dec., 1596, M.A. 10 April, 1600, licenced to preach 10 Nov., 1606, B.D. 11 May, 1608, rector of Denbury, Devon, 1606, and of Authorpe, co. Lincoln, 1617, canon of Exeter 1630. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Briscoe, John, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1602, bencher 1620, Lent reader 1623, treasurer 1627. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Reynolds, William (Reinolds), of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 15.
Baldwin, Thomas, of Middlesex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 17.

Ruggle, George, y.s. Thomas, of Lavenham, Suffolk; admitted St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 26 June, 1589, scholar Trinity Coll. 1593, B.A. 1593-4, M.A. 1597, fellow Clare Hall circa 1598 (incorporated 30 Aug., 1605), taxor 1604; dramatic satirist, author of "Ignoramus," a comedy played before James I. at Cambridge 8 March, 1614-15; born at Lavenham Nov., 1575, made his will 6 Sept., 1622, proved 3 Nov., following. See Fasti, i. 310.

Blickearde, Stukeley, of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1593, aged 17, B.A. 9 July, 1596; fellow Merton Coll. 1597, M.A. 4 July, 1601, B.D. (sup. 4 May) 1612, incorp. at Cambridge as M.A. 1612, rector of Mount Bures, Essex, 1610, until his death in 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., iv. 276.

Roberts, Thomas, of Essex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 20; B.A. 16 Feb., 1595-6, M.A. 4 July, 1599; student of Middle Temple 1593, as son and heir of Thomas, of Little Braxted, Essex, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
White, William, of Bucks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1593, aged 15, B.A. 8 Dec., 1596, M.A. 14 May, 1601, B.D. 7 June, 1608, D.D. 2 July, 1612, licenced to preach 13 April, 1619.
Stonor, (Sir) Henry, of Oxon, arm. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 22 June, 1593, aged 14; (1s. Sir Francis, of Stonor, Oxon); knighted at Whitehall 23 July, 1603.
Jones, William, of Surrey, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 13; perhaps B.A. from Balliol Coll. 23 June, 1598, M.A. 7 July, 1608, then in orders.
Edmonds, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1593, aged 17; B.A. 15 Dec., 1597; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 8 July, 1602.
Lenthall, Edmund, of Oxon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1593, aged 15. See also John 1601.
Martin, Thomas, of London (city), gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1593, aged 21.
Jones, Walter, of co. Brecon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 Feb., 1593-4, aged 15.

Calverte, George, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 14; B.A. 23 Feb., 1596-7, a student in municipal law, created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605, entered Lincoln's Inn 1595 (son of Leonard Calvert, by Alice his wife, daughter of John Crosland, of Crosland), born at Kipling, Yorks, secretary to Sir Robert Cecill, secretary of state; a clerk of the council, knighted at Hampton Court 29 Sept., 1617, secretary of state to the King 15 Feb., 1618-19-24, M.P. Bossiney 1609-11, Yorkshire 1621-2, Oxford University 1624-5, created Baron Baltimore 16 Feb., 1624, obtained a grant of Maryland from Charles I., father of Cecil last named, and died 15 April, 1632, buried in St. Dunstan-inthe-West. See Ath., ii. 522; & D.N.B.

Lovet, (Sir) Robert, of Bucks, gen. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14; student of Middle Temple 1600 (as only son of Francis, of Liscombe, Bucks), knighted 22 Nov., 1606, sheriff of Bucks 1608; died 1643; father of John 1637. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Allen, James, of London, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 15; B.A. 25 Feb., 1597-8; one of these names rector of Little Henny, Essex, 1621. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Tuer, Daniel, of Middlesex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 16, B.A. 30 May, 1598.
Hutchinson, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 18.

Methwin, Anthony, of Somerset, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 20; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 24 April, 1599; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 21 Jan., 1601-2, B.D. 31 May, 1609; canon of Lichfield 1611, vicar of Frome Selwood 1606, and rector of Lamyat (both Somerset), 1614, until his death 6 July, 1640, aged 66, buried at Frome, his will dated 12 April, and proved 12 Aug., 1640; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Cottington, Edward, of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 14.
Cottington, James, of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 17.
Everard, Daniel, of Essex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1595, aged 18.

Unton, Thomas (Umpton), of Salop, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 26 Nov., 1596, aged 14, B.A. 4 July, 1599; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1608, as Thomas Unton, of Drayton, Salop, gent; father of George and Thomas next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Whistler, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 17, B.A. 17 Feb., 1600-1; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1611, treasurer 1640 (as son and heir of Hugh, of Milton Parva, Oxon, gent.), recorder of Oxford and M.P. 1624-5, 1625, 1626, 1628-9, and 1640 (L.P.), till disabled in 1644; brother of Henry and Hugh. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Chetwod, Richard, of Northants, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. Jan., 1597-8, aged 17; of Badby and Newnham, Northants, admitted to Gray's Inn 1598 (as son and heir of Richard, sheriff of Northants, who was knighted 9 July, 1603), died before 1635, father of Valentine, half brother of Robert, Thomas, and Tobias, 1607. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Bond, Richard, of Oxon, 'cler. fil.' Trinity Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 16; B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2, M.A. 10 July, 1607, then in orders, one of these names rector of Frittenden, Kent, 1620, until sequestered in 1646. See Add. M.S., 15,670, p. 191; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Makblythe, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Dec., 1597, aged 16.
Hughes, Roger, of co. Brecon, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 12.
Shrigley, Edmund, of co. Stafford, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 18.
Powell, Griffin, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. — Jan., 1597-8, aged 16.

Whis(t)ler, Henry, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 5 May, 1598, aged 12, B.A. 8 May 1604, M.A. 12 May, 1608, B.D. 23 Nov., 1615; licenced to preach 23 Nov., 1615, rector of Burghclere, Hants, 1617, of Witney, Oxon, 1618, and of Wittenham Abbots, Berks, 1618-58, until his death 24 Aug., 1672; buried in the chancel of Witney church; brother of Hugh next, and of John 1597. See Ath. iii. 962; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Biddulph, Michael, of co. Stafford, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 5 May, 1598, aged 17; a student of the Middle Temple 1599, as son and heir of Simon, of Elmhurst, co. Stafford, gent., M.P. Lichfield Dec., 1646 (L.P.) till secluded in 1648, and died 28 Jan., 1657-8, probably brother of Walter 1605, and father of Anthony 1635. See Foster's Baronetage & Inns of Court Reg.

Coventry, Vincent, of Oxon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 30 June, 1603, M.A. 12 May, 1608, rector of Begbrooke, Oxon, 1614, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Walk(e)den, Matthew, of Bucks, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 May, 1598, aged 15, B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2, M.A. 20 June, 1605, then in orders; rector of Pitchcott, Bucks, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Palmes, Thomas, of Hants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 May, 1598, aged 15; B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2, M.A. 12 Dec., 1604, as "eq. aur. fil.;" perhaps brother of Francis 1591, and of William next-named.
Cotes, John, of Salop, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 May, 1598, aged 19; (called Coates in Mat. Reg.).
Potter, Simon, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 5 May, 1598, aged 17, B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2.
Marston, Gabriel, of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 15; Maston in Mat. Reg.
Lane, John, of Middx., gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 May, 1598, aged 14.
Parsons, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 5 May, 1598, aged 17.

Votier, Daniel, of Middlesex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 16, B.A. 30 June, 1603, M.A. 17 June, 1607; rector of St. Petercheap, London, 1615, until sequestered in 1645 by the Westminster assembly of divines; father of the next. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 60; St. Paul's S. R. 30; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Spencer, Abraham, of co. Hereford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 16, B.A. 7 July, 1603, M.A. 10 July, 1607; vicar of St. Albans St. Michael's 1617, until sequestered in 1645 by the Westminster assembly, vicar also of Elstree, Herts, 1618. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 238; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Fenn, Humphrey, of co. Warwick, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1601-10, B.A. 12 Dec., 1604, M.A. 8 July, 1607. See Bloxam, v. 1; & D.N.B.
Annesley, Ralph, of Bucks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 16; B.A. 15 Dec., 1603, M.A. 10 July, 1607, rector of Easter Keal, co. Lincoln, 1612.
Chimley, Theophilus (Chymligh), of co. York, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19; vicar of Hornsey, Yorks, 1607. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hooke, John, of co. Warwick, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 16; B.A. from New Coll. 28 Feb., 1605-6.
Turner, Roger, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 5 March, 1602-3.
Percy, Joseline, of Yorks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 15; of Beverley (s. Alan); died 1652-3.
Wirdnam, Francis, of Berks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 26 Oct., 1599, aged 14.
Harvey, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 17.
Greene, Henry, of co. York, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1599, aged 17.

Byker, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Sept., 1600, aged 17; B.A. 8 May, 1604, M.A. 17 June, 1607, rector of Frankton, co. Warwick, 1626, vicar of Dunchurch 1627, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Add. MS., 15,670.

Dell, John, of Cornwall, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Sept., 1600, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 23 Oct., 1604, M.A. 18 June, 1607, rector of Gerrans, Cornwall, 1611, father of Richard 1639. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Vernay, (Sir) Francis, of London, equitis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Sept., 1600, aged 15; of Penley, Herts (s. Sir Edmund); died 6 Sept., 1615; halfbrother of Edmund 1604.
Cheyne, Francis, of Herts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Sept., 1600, aged 26; of Lincoln's Inn 1597, possibly father of Francis and Charles. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Poulett, Amys (Powlett), of co. Gloucester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 17; of Thornbury, co. Gloucester; son of Nicholas, of Minty, same county.
Chimley, Thomas (Chimligh), of co. York, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1600, vicar of Atwick, Yorks, 1624. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Throckmorton, George, of Bucks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 17 Oct., 1600, B.A. 30 June, 1603. See Henry 1598, and Berry's Bucks, i. 271.
Newman, Richard, of co. Worcester, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 July, 1600, aged 17; B.A. 8 May, 1604.
Hol(l)iwell, John, of Salop, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 Sept., 1600, aged 18, B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2.
Swayne, Philip, of co. Hereford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 Feb., 1600-1, B.A. 12 Dec., 1604.
Pratt, Francis, of Herts, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1600, B.A. 8 May, 1604.
Crabbe, Launcelot, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Sept., 1600, aged 19.
Lilgrave, Dudley, of Bucks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 19 Sept., 1600, aged 27.

St. John, John, of Wilts, equitis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 April, 1601; of Lydiard Tregoze, Wilts, and of Battersea and Wandsworth, Surrey; student of Lincoln's Inn 1604 (s. Sir John), knighted 2 Feb., 1608-9, created a baronet 22 May, 1611, M.P. Wilts 1624-5; father of Edward 1634. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Palmes, William, of Southants, equitis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1604, as of Lancelevy, Hants, gen. (s. Sir Francis); perhaps brother of Thomas and Francis 1591. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ernle, Walter (Earnly), of Wilts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; student of Lincoln's Inn 1605; probably son of Michael, of Borton, parish of Cannings, Wilts, and died s.p. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Combes, Francis, of Herts, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 17; of the Middle Temple 1602, as son and heir of Francis, of Hempstead, Herts, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Douche, William, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 19; rector of Warmwell, Dorset, 1613, and of Stalbridge, Dorset, 1621. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Greenwood, Thomas (Greenewood), of Somerset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 14, B.A. 31 Jan., 1604-5; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 31 May, 1609.
Cardrowe, Henry, of Dorset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1601, aged 16; B.A. 5 July, 1604 (his parents were dead before March, 1603-4).
Percy, Thomas, of Yorks, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 26 June, 1601, aged 12; B.A. 17 Dec., 1605, M.A. 8 July, 1608; brother of Robert.
Hare, Anthony, of London, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 15; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 23 Jan., 1604-5.
Biddle, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 June, 1601, aged 15 (18), called Beedle in Mat. Reg.
Goare, John, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 18; B.A. 20 June, 1605.
Ambrose, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 18.
Cary, Peter, of Isle of Guernsey, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 3 April, 1601, aged 17.
Blacklege, John, of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16.
Butts, James, of Suffolk, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1601-2, aged 14.
Dynham, John, of Oxon, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 16.

Wentworth, Thomas, baron Wentworth, of Nettlestede. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 12 Nov., 1602, aged 11; 4th baron, created baron Wentworth and earl of Cleveland 7 Feb., 1625, captain of the band of gentlemen pensioners; died 25 March, 1667; brother of Henry 1602.

Andrewes, John, of co. Somerset. pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 9 July, 1602, aged 19; B.A. 17 Dec., 1605, 'a painful preacher,' seems to have been minister and preacher at Berwick Basset, Wilts. See Ath., ii. 493; & D.N.B.

Samon, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 5 Nov., 1602, aged 14; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 31 Jan., 1605-6, M.A. 22 May, 1612; perhaps minister of Badsey, co. Worcester; father of the next-named.

Egerton, Thomas, of Bucks, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 Feb., 1602-3, aged 15; B.A. 10 Nov., 1606, M.A. 29 Nov., 1609, rector of Adstock, Bucks, 1622; father of William 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gould, William, of Dorset, gent. Trinity Coll. matric. 5 Nov., 1602, aged 19; possibly student of Middle Temple 1605, as son and heir of William, of Awlston, Wilts, gent. See Foster's Inns of Couri Reg.
Campion, George, of Northants, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 7 May, 1602, aged 14; B.A. 5 May, 1607, M.A. 18 April, 1611, rector of Sywell, Northants, 1612. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wentworth, Henry, baronis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 12 Nov., 1602, aged "8"; majorgeneral in the service of Charles I. (s. Henry, baron Wentworth); died 1644; brother of Thomas 1602.
Denton, Thomas, of Suffolk, 'Gener. Conditionis.' Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1602, aged 26. See Anthony 1578, and John 1599.
King, John, of Norfolk, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 18; B.A. 17 Dec., 1605.
Fox, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1602, aged 18; B.A. 18 Feb., 1608-9.
Swayne, William (Swaine), of co. Derby, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 Feb., 1602-3, aged 15.
Portingtown, Robert, of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 Feb., 1602-3, aged 16.
Bowen, William, of London, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 30 April, 1602, aged 17.
Pymme, John, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 7 May, 1602, aged 14.

Tippinge, John, of Oxon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 10 Feb., 1603-4, aged 14, B.A. 8 July, 1606; of Whitfield, Oxon, student of Lincoln's Inn 1608 (s. of Sir George), buried in Whitchurch 25 Dec., 1618, brother of Samuel 1615, and father of Thomas 1631. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wilcox, Abel (Willcox), of Northants, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 15, B.A. 10 Nov., 1606; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 20 June, 1609; died 28 March, 1613, M.I. St. Peter-inthe-East, Oxford. See Hearne, i. 137.

Cartmell, Nicholas, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' Trinity Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 14, B.A. 5 May, 1607; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 6 July, 1610, vicar of Daventry, Northants, 1623. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Glover, Edward, of Essex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 16; B.A. 10 Nov., 1606, M.A. 27 June, 1609, incorporated at Cambridge 1617, rector of Lamarsh, Essex, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brooke, Richard, of co. Worcester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 Feb., 1603-4, aged 17; B.A. 5 1607, M.A. 18 April, 1611, B.D. 10 May, 1619. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wilmer, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 18; perhaps student of Lincoln's Inn 1603, as of co. Derby, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bradshaw, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 6 May, 1603, aged 15, B.A. 5 May, 1607; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 6 July, 1610.
Browne, Thomas, of London, 'cler. fil.' Trinity Coll., matric. 23 March, 1603-4, aged 17; B.A. 2 June, 1608, M.A. 5 May, 1612.
Anneslay, John, of Notts, arm. fil. nat. max. Trinity Coll., matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 18.
Greene, Raiphe, of Yorks, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 15.
Carew, Matthew, of Essex, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 May, 1603, aged 16.
Hopkins, Samuel, of Essex, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 6 May, 1603, aged 16.
Rufin, Henry, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 6 May, 1603, aged 16.

Ironside, Gilbert, s. (Ralph), of co. Gloucester, S.T.B. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 June, 1604, aged 15; scholar 1605, B.A. 2 June, 1608, M.A. 5 May, 1612, fellow 1613, B.D. 1 July, 1619 (incorporated at Cambridge 1620), D.D. by diploma 1 Dec., 1660; born at Hawksbury 25 Nov., 1588, rector of Winterbourne Steepleton, Dorset, 1619, and of Winterborne Abbas 1625, rector of Yeovilton, Somerset, 1662, canon of York 1660-2, bishop of Bristol 13 Jan., 1660-1, until his death 19 Sept., 1671; father of the next-named, of Thomas 1641, and of Ralph 1650. See Ath. iii. 940; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Harington, John (Haryngton), of Somerset, militis fil. nat. max. Trinity Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1604, aged 15; perhaps migrated to Cambridge 1607, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1615, treasurer 1651 (as son and heir of Sir John, of Kelston, Somerset, kt.); M.P. Somerset Dec., 1645, till void April, 1646, re-elected July, 1646, until secluded, Dec., 1648; his will dated 21 April and proved 6 July, 1654; died before 31 May, 1654; father of John 1640. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Breedon, Zacheus, of Bucks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 30 March, 1604, aged 16; B.A. 9 Nov., 1607 (M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 14 July, 1612); B.D. 1 July, 1619, rector of Croughton, Northants, 1631, buried there 9 July, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Addridge, Robert, of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 15; B.A. 9 Nov., 1607, M.A. from St. Edmund Hall, 15 April, 1611, rector of Saltford, Somerset, 1611, until his death in 1615. See Weaver.

Randes, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 14 Dec., 1604, aged 14; B.A. 2 June, 1608, M.A. 29 April, 1612, B.D. 1 July, 1619; licenced to preach 2 July, 1622; incorporated at Cambridge 1621.

Horsey, (Sir) George, of Dorset, militis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 16, of Clifton and Melcombe Horsey, Dorset (s. Sir Ralph), knighted at Whitehall 10 Feb., 1631, or 16 Feb., 1618.
Winder, Samuel, of Berks, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 18 Jan., 1604-5, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 25 June, 1608; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 27 June, 1611, then in orders.
Staverton, Deodatus, of Northants, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 28 June, 1604, aged 16, student of Lincoln's Inn 1606, as of Berks. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dorington, Francis, of co. Stafford, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 18; of Gray's Inn 1607. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Bathurst, George, of London, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 14; a student of Lincoln's Inn, 1607, as of Kent, gent.
Paston, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 1 March, 1604-5, aged 16; B.A. 9 Nov., 1607.
Spurlinge, Edward, of Herts, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 6 July, 1604, aged 18.
Pollard, John, of Dorset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1604, aged 16.
Feilde, Francis, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 30 March, 1604, aged 16.

Carrill, (Sir) Richard (Carill), of London, mil. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1605, aged 15; B.A. 11 May, 1608, of the Inner Temple 1605, as of Harting, Sussex, gent., and grandson of a bencher (son of Sir Edward and grandson of John, serjt.-atlaw 1540), knighted at Greenwich 26 May, 1615, and died 16 Sept., 1616, aged 26, half-brother of Thomas 1588. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Powlet, (Sir) Hector (Pawlet), born in Southants, s. (William 3rd) marquis. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 July, 1605, aged 16; knighted 25 Aug., 1619; brother of Hercules, John, and William 1595, all natural sons of William 3rd marquis. See Collins' Peerage (Brydges, ii. 375), and Berry's Hants, 268.

Fleetwood, Arthur (Fletwoode), of Bucks, militis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 May, 1605, aged 17, B.A. 9 Nov., 1607; incorporated at Cambridge 1608; student of Gray's Inn 1609, as son and heir of Sir George, of the Vache, Bucks: dead before 1620. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Biddulph, Walter, of co. Stafford, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 24 May, 1605, aged 18; B.A. 18 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 26 June, 1611, B.D. 14 Dec., 1620, rector of Rolleston, co. Stafford, 1615, possibly a brother of Michael 1598.

Fleetwoode, Edward, of Bucks, "armiger." Trinity Coll., matric. 24 May, 1605, aged 34; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1594, as 2nd son of William, serjeant-at-law; probably brother of Francis. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Wynn, John, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 15 Nov., 1605, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1608, as of Conway, co. Carnarvon, perhaps knighted 22 May, 1613. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Williams, Henry, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. entry 15 Nov., 1605, aged 14, student of Lincoln's Inn 1609, as son of William, of Vaenol, co. Carnarvon, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Vaughan, John, of co. Hereford, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 26 July, 1605, aged 16; perhaps student of Gray's Inn 14 or 15 March, 1608-9. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Seymour, Thomas, of Southants, militis fil. Trinity Coll., matric. entry dated 26 July, 1605, aged 12; see Edward 1594, and Henry 1604.
Reynolds, Rice (subs. "Ryse Raynolds"), of Salop, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 16; B.A. 19 Nov., 1608.
Reasonn, John, of Oxon, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1605, aged 17; B.A. 18 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 5 May, 1612.
Williams, Thomas, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1605, aged 16.
Cardrow, Matthew, of Dorset, gent. Trinity Coll., matric. 22 Nov., 1605, aged 17.

Skinner, Robert, of Northants, cler. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 July, 1606, aged 15, B.A. 14 June, 1610, fellow 1613, M.A. 25 June, 1614 (incorporated at Cambridge 1615), B.D. 19 April, 1621 (re-incorporated at Cambridge 1621), licenced to preach 14 Jan., 1621-2, created D.D. 13 Aug. 1636(s. Edmund, of Pisford, Northants, cler.), preacher of St. Gregory's, near St. Paul's, London, 1621-30, chaplain in ordinary to Charles I., rector of Pisford, 1628-35, rector of Launton, Oxon, 1631, and of Greens Norton, Northants, 1636, until sequestered 1643, sequestered also from the vicarage of Cuddesden 1646, and from the rectory of Beckenham 1647; bishop of Bristol 1637-41, and of Oxford 1641, imprisoned in the Tower 1641, restored 1660, bishop of Worcester 1663, until his death 14 June 1670; father of Matthew 1639. See Ath. iv. 842; Fasti, i. 489; Lansdowne MS. 986, ff. 135, 137; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Harwarde, John, of Southants, S.T.B. fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 11 July, 1606, aged 16, B.A. 14 June, 1610; M.A. from Hart Hall 28 April, 1613; one of these names sequestered to vicarage of Canewdon, Essex, 1645; brother of Thomas 1608. See Add. MS. 15,669, pp. 190, 230.

Aish, Robert (Aysh), of Somerset, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 Feb., 1606-7, aged 19; B.A. 3 Nov., 1610, M.A. 10 June, 1613, preb. of Bath and Wells 1625, vicar of Clevedon, Somerset, 1626. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Gulson, Anthony, of co. Leicester, pleb. Trinity Coll., matric. 16 May, 1606, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1616, as son and heir of Humphrey of Howby, co. Leicester, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bourne, Gilbert, of Somerset, s.t.d., fil. Trinity Coll., matric. 13 Feb., 1606-7, aged 15; B.A. 14 June, 1610, M.A. 8 July, 1613, rector of Yeovilton 1616, canon of Wells 1618. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Va(u)ghan, Benjamin, of London, arm. Trinity Coll., matric. 20 March, 1606-7, aged 17, B.A. 28 Nov., 1610; M.A. from Broadgates Hall 17 June, 1613; rector of Charlinch, Somerset, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
